group_analyses.risk_assessment: High risk due to large number of repos (14), potential for language-specific build conflicts, runtime dependency mismatches, and loss of language-specific tooling configurations. Risk of breaking cross-language interfaces.
key_files_to_preserve: flux-py: Python bindings, pip packaging configs, PyPI deployment scripts
flux-js: npm package.json, WebAssembly bindings, browser compatibility tests
flux-zig: build.zig, low-level memory management modules
flux-wasm: WebAssembly compilation configs, browser runtime shims
flux-java: Maven/Gradle configs, JNI bindings
flux-llama: ML model integration, quantization scripts
flux-swarm: distributed coordination logic, consensus algorithms
flux-simulator: simulation test suites, performance benchmarks
flux-visualizer: UI components, visualization libraries
flux-ide: IDE plugin configurations, language server protocols
flux-coverage: code coverage tools, instrumentation scripts
flux-cross-assembler: cross-compilation toolchains, target-specific optimizations
flux-evolve-py: evolutionary algorithm implementations, training pipelines
group_analyses.reasoning: Large but self-contained; should be done after foundational infrastructure merges but before dependent groups.

group_analyses.risk_assessment: Medium-high risk due to multiple language implementations (Rust, C, Go, CUDA, Zig) that may have conflicting build systems. CUDA dependencies pose hardware/compilation risks. Risk of breaking performance-critical native bindings.
key_files_to_preserve: holodeck-rust: Cargo.toml, unsafe FFI modules, memory safety audits
holodeck-c: CMakeLists.txt, low-level graphics drivers, hardware abstraction layer
holodeck-go: Go modules, concurrent runtime scheduler
holodeck-cuda: .cu files, kernel launch configurations, GPU memory managers
holodeck-zig: comptime configurations, cross-platform build definitions
group_analyses.reasoning: Performance-sensitive with GPU dependencies; merge after core runtime groups but before higher-level tooling.

group_analyses.risk_assessment: Medium risk. Integration risks with GitHub API changes, authentication token management, and agent communication protocols. Breaking changes could disrupt fleet management operations.
key_files_to_preserve: fleet-agent-api: OpenAPI specs, gRPC/protobuf definitions, client SDKs
fleet-github-app: GitHub App manifest, webhook handlers, OAuth flow implementations
group_analyses.reasoning: Infrastructure foundation; should be merged early to stabilize platform for other merges.

group_analyses.risk_assessment: Low-medium risk. Mostly training/onboarding content rather than production code. Risk of losing pedagogical structure or example configurations.
key_files_to_preserve: git-agent-standard: Git workflow tutorials, standard operating procedures
dockside-exam: Certification test suites, skill assessment rubrics
greenhorn-onboarding: Getting started guides, environment setup scripts
group_analyses.reasoning: Documentation/training focused; low priority for production stability.

group_analyses.risk_assessment: High risk. Core inter-system communication layer. Breaking changes could cascade through entire fleet. Protocol versioning critical.
key_files_to_preserve: flux-baton: Handoff protocols, state transfer serialization
mesosynchronous: Timing coordination algorithms, deadline schedulers
fleet-liaison-tender: Service discovery registries, health check endpoints
group_analyses.reasoning: Critical communication infrastructure; must be merged first to ensure stable foundation.

group_analyses.risk_assessment: Medium risk. Protocol specification and runtime separation could cause version drift. Risk of breaking backward compatibility in distributed systems.
key_files_to_preserve: cocapn-runtime: Protocol state machines, message routers, connection pools
commodore-protocol: Wire format specifications, encryption handshakes
group_analyses.reasoning: Protocol layer; merge after I2I but before application layers that depend on it.

group_analyses.risk_assessment: Low-medium risk. Skill definitions and agent capabilities. Risk of losing modular skill isolation, but low production impact.
key_files_to_preserve: flux-skills: Core skill libraries, capability manifests
agent-skills: Agent-specific skill implementations, proficiency tests
group_analyses.reasoning: Application logic; depends on stable runtime and infrastructure.

group_analyses.risk_assessment: Low risk. Research code with fewer production dependencies. Risk of losing experimental branches or research provenance.
key_files_to_preserve: abstraction-planes: Theoretical frameworks, conceptual diagrams
isa-v3-draft: Instruction set architecture specifications
edge-research-relay: Experimental result datasets, reproducibility scripts

group_analyses.risk_assessment: Low risk. Mostly static assets and translations. Risk of bloating repo size with binary assets, localization sync issues.
key_files_to_preserve: All language repos: locale files, translation memory, RTL layout configurations
fleet-json-a2a: Automated translation pipelines, string extraction tools
group_analyses.reasoning: Static assets; can be merged last with minimal impact.

parallel_plan.rationale: I2I is foundational dependency. Fleet-infra depends on stable I2I. Flux-runtime and cocapn can merge in parallel as they're largely independent. Holodeck and skills depend on runtime. Final phase contains all independent/low-risk groups.

systemic_risks: Version pinning conflicts across merged repos
Loss of commit history for absorbed repos
mitigation_recommendations: Implement phased CI migration with parallel runs
Preserve original repos as read-only archives
Use git-subtree instead of git-merge for cleaner history
Establish merge validation gates with integration tests
